violations.text: Milk chest #3 was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Cheese sticks were observed held between 42-43 DF and were moved to properly functioning refrigeration. Yogurt was observed between 45-47 DF and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The floor in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC FMC license expired. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Lactic acid sanitizer solution was in excess of 2258 parts per million (ppm). A lactic acid sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label (704-1875ppm). PIC refilled 3 bay sanitizer solution and retested at 1875ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the a scoop/ladle have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris. PIC placed in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The food prep sink in the kitchen area has an air break where an air gap is required. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025
Milk cooler door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024
The handwashing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2024
Bread package was observed gnawed. Bread was voluntarily disposed by PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024
Deli meat was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by adulterated food and droppings in back storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024
Milk cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2024

Yogurt was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Product disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
